text: **Bruna:** Hi Anna.
**Anna:** Hi Bruno, what's {up|調子/状況|/ʌp/|What's up with you today?}?
**Bruna:** I thought we could meet for {lunch|昼食|/lʌntʃ/|Let's have lunch together.} today.
**Bruna:** I wish I could, but I can't.
**Anna:** I have to {research|調査する/研究する|/rɪˈsɜːrtʃ/|I will research this topic.} Peru for a story.
**Bruna:** How's it going?
**Anna:** Not so good.
**Anna:** I've been {reading|読書する|/ˈriːdɪŋ/|I am reading a book.} about Peru and listening to Peruvian music.
**Anna:** I really want to {understand|理解する|/ˌʌndərˈstænd/|Do you understand me?} Peru.
**Anna:** I hope it's enough.
**Bruna:** You should {visit|訪問する|/ˈvɪzɪt/|I will visit my friend.} Peru.
**Anna:** I wish You can.
**Anna:** Bruna, I don't have the money or time to go to Peru.
**Anna:** My {deadline|締め切り|/ˈdeadlaɪn/|The deadline is tomorrow.} is tomorrow.
**Bruna:** No.
**Bruna:** I don't mean to really go there.
**Bruna:** The {museum|博物館|/mjuˈziːəm/|I visited a museum today.} where I work is having a Peruvian festival today.
**Bruna:** Anna?
**Bruna:** Anna, are you there?
**Anna:** Hello again, Professor Bot here.
**Anna:** Today we will hear about wishes and hopes.
**Anna:** Bruna asked Anna to go to lunch, but she answered,
**Bruna:** I wish I could, but I can't.
**Anna:** Listen for more hope and wish sentences.
**Bruna:** Hi Anna.
**Bruna:** Hi Bruno.
**Anna:** Thanks for telling me about this {event|出来事/イベント|/ɪˈvent/|This is an important event.}
**Bruna:** Sure.
**Bruna:** It's much better than {staring|じっと見つめる|/ˈstɛrɪŋ/|Stop staring at me.} at your computer.
**Anna:** I hope it helps with your story.
**Anna:** I'm sure it will.
**Anna:** So what should we see first?
**Bruna:** {Dance|踊り/ダンス|/dæns/|Let's dance together.} and {music|音楽|/ˈmjuːzɪk/|I love classical music.} are very important to Peruvian culture.
**Bruna:** So let's watch the dancers.
**Anna:** Great, let's go!
**Bruna:** There's this very special Peruvian {dance|踊り/ダンス} called {marinera|マリネラ} {nortenia|北部の}.
**Anna:** I love the marinera nortina.
**Bruna:** Marinera Nortena.
**Anna:** Marinera Nortina.
**Bruna:** No.
**Bruna:** Marinera Nortena.
**Anna:** I love the {marriage|結婚} dance.
**Anna:** Bruna, I read about this dance, but it is much more {romantic|ロマンチック} in person.
**Bruna:** The dancers are giving {lessons|レッスン/授業} after their {performance|公演/演技}.
**Bruna:** You should try it.
**Anna:** I'd love to
**Anna:** But I'm not ready to get married.
**Anna:** I mean, one day I hope to meet the right guy.
**Anna:** We'll buy matching {motorcycles|オートバイ} and
**Anna:** {Ride|乗る} into the sunset, the wind blowing through our hair.
**Bruna:** Anna, Anna, the dance just shows the {courtship|求愛} tradition.
**Anna:** It doesn't mean you will get married
**Anna:** Oh.
**Anna:** Well, I'll try anyway.
**Bruna:** Don't forget, this {festival|祭り/フェスティバル} also shows Peruvian art.
**Anna:** I hope I have time to see everything.
**Anna:** Anna, I wish I could join you, but I have some work to do.
**Anna:** Of course, of course.
**Anna:** You go back to work.
**Anna:** I'll check out the art.
**Bruna:** Okay.
**Bruna:** I'm hoping to {finish|終える/完了する} in about an hour.
**Bruna:** Let's meet then.
**Anna:** Great.
**Anna:** See you then.
**Anna:** Yeah.
**Anna:** Did you notice any {sentences|文章} with hope or wish?
**Anna:** Anna says, One day I {hope|望む} to meet the right guy.
**Anna:** That's an example of using hope with an {infinitive|不定詞} to talk about doing something in the future.
**Anna:** Keep watching.
**Anna:** Oh wow, I can make my own {Peruvian|ペルーの} art.
**Bruna:** Ah, how's it going?
**Bruna:** Hi Bruno.
**Bruna:** You know you're in the {kids|子供の} section, right?
**Anna:** I'm in the art making section.
**Bruna:** I see that.
**Bruna:** Your art is very nice.
**Bruna:** I {wish|願う} I could do art like that.
**Anna:** Oh no, this one's not mine
**Anna:** This is mine.
**Anna:** Wow.
**Bruna:** Really?
**Bruna:** That's really nice.
**Anna:** Thanks.
**Anna:** And thanks for telling me about this {festival|祭り/祭典}.
**Anna:** I learned so much about Peru.
**Anna:** Oh, we still have time.
**Bruna:** Time for what?
**Anna:** Time for {Marinera|マリネラ} Norteña.
**Anna:** Do you remember what Anna said?
**Anna:** I really want to {understand|理解する/分かる|/ˌʌndərˈstænd/|I want to understand this book.} Peru.
**Anna:** I hope you {understand|理解する/分かる|/ˌʌndərˈstænd/|I hope you understand my point.} Peru a little better now too.
**Anna:** And I wish I could {talk|話す/語る|/tɔːk/|Let's talk about our plans.} with you more about grammar.
**Anna:** But that's all the {time|時間/時|/taɪm/|We have a short time to finish.} we have today.
**Anna:** Go to the {website|ウェブサイト/ウェブページ|/ˈwebsaɪt/|Visit our website for more information.} to learn more.
